title: afzelia pectinata
intro: Afzelia pectinata, also known as the African teak or doka, is a deciduous tree native to tropical Africa. The bark and roots of Afzelia pectinata possess medicinal properties, containing compounds such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and tannins, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The tree is valued for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and treating fever, as well as its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Afzelia pectinata is often prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
